title: Job Application Assistant
description: Built a local Chrome extension and FastAPI backend that extracts job opportunities, drafts auditable applications with Codex, exports cover-letter PDFs, and keeps a SQLite application ledger.
seoTitle: Job Application Assistant Case Study | Auditable Local Application Workflow
seoDescription: A case study on Job Application Assistant, a local Chrome extension and Python backend for structured job extraction, Codex-backed drafts, cover-letter PDFs, and SQLite application tracking.
constraints: The tool handles sensitive job descriptions, personal context, draft text, application history, and generated PDFs, so the workflow needed to stay local-first and explicit about what is stored.
Job-board extraction had to avoid broad page-text guessing. Missing fields needed warnings instead of invented context.
problem: Applying to jobs was split across job boards, extension popups, local portfolio context, cover-letter drafts, PDFs, and a separate application tracking habit.
The bottleneck was not only writing faster. It was keeping the job snapshot, selected proof points, drafting strategy, final PDF, and application ledger tied to evidence.
solution: Built a Manifest V3 Chrome extension that extracts normalized opportunity snapshots from supported job pages, lets the user review them, and sends them to a local FastAPI backend.

Indexed local profile, resume, offers, and project proof points into a structured context bundle, then ran one Codex draft pass that returns the draft plus selected angle, selected and rejected projects, decisions, claims, and warnings.
Persisted draft jobs, draft JSON, generated cover-letter PDFs, and application records in SQLite, with lookup endpoints so the extension can show already-applied status.
result: Created a public local-first workflow for application drafting where the applicant-facing text, audit trail, generated PDF, and application ledger remain inspectable on the machine.
Separated extraction, context selection, drafting, PDF export, and application logging into explicit contracts covered by tests instead of one fragile browser script.
Extended the workflow beyond drafting into supported apply-enabled Dice, Indeed, and LinkedIn flows while preserving manual review points and platform-confirmed logging.
deliveryHighlights: Chrome extension side panel for extraction review, draft requests, PDF actions, already-applied checks, and apply-enabled posting lists.
FastAPI backend with async draft jobs, context reindexing, draft lookup, application lookup, dashboard, and PDF export endpoints.
SQLite stores draft requests, draft JSON, job stages, timing metadata, and application records deduplicated by normalized source URL.
Contract tests cover extraction modules, prompt inputs, Codex JSON parsing, application storage, dashboard rendering, PDF export, and extension JavaScript syntax.
